name: Demopolis
description_short: Demopolis Federal Credit Union is a community-focused credit union established in 1966, serving over 600 members in Demopolis, Alabama with $2.2M in assets.
description_long:
Demopolis Federal Credit Union was established in 1966 and is headquartered in Demopolis, Alabama. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates as a member-owned financial cooperative designed to serve the local community. The institution has maintained a presence in Demopolis for nearly 60 years, building relationships with residents and local businesses.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking services including savings accounts, auto loans, personal loans, mortgages, and money market accounts. Members can access e-statements, online banking, and a mobile app for both iOS and Android devices. The institution also provides financial counseling, financial education resources, and various calculators to help members make informed decisions about their finances. Share draft accounts and share certificates (CDs) are available, though current product pages indicate limited activity in some account categories.

Demopolis FCU distinguishes itself through personalized, community-oriented service. Member reviews emphasize the credit union's friendly approach and willingness to treat members as individuals rather than account numbers. The institution maintains a single main office location at 106 E Washington Street and currently operates with limited branch infrastructure—1 branch and 0 ATMs. The credit union prioritizes member relationships and local financial accessibility over expansion, serving a membership base of 600+ individuals.

As a small credit union with $2.2M in assets, Demopolis FCU is well-suited for members seeking personalized service and community banking but may have limitations in product sophistication, technology offerings, and service hours compared to larger institutions. The lobby is currently closed according to the website, which suggests operational or staffing constraints. Prospective members should verify current hours and service availability directly before attempting to conduct in-person transactions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Member-centric approach with personalized service—reviews specifically note treating members as individuals, not account numbers
Established 1966—nearly 60 years of community banking history and stability in Demopolis
Competitive auto loan options for new and used vehicles with rates available
Mobile banking app available on both iOS and Android platforms for convenient access
Free financial counseling and financial education resources for members
Mortgage lending available with calculators to help members evaluate options
Perfect 5.0-star rating based on member reviews praising friendly and helpful service
Online banking and e-statements for remote account management
cons: Only 1 branch location and 0 ATMs—extremely limited physical access and no surcharge-free ATM network
Lobby currently closed—website indicates service availability constraints or staffing issues
Very small asset base ($2.2M)—may limit product offerings, loan approval amounts, or technology capabilities
Limited membership base (600 members)—smaller pool may result in fewer community resources or negotiating power with partners
No indication of competitive rates or rate comparisons to larger credit unions—difficult for members to benchmark value
best_for: Local Demopolis residents seeking personalized, community-focused banking relationships
Members who prioritize friendly service and individual attention over extensive branch networks
Borrowers seeking auto loans or mortgages from a local, mission-driven financial institution
Savers who value financial education and counseling services from a trusted local source
diagnosis: Demopolis Federal Credit Union is best for Demopolis-area residents who value personalized, community banking and don't require extensive ATM networks or multiple branch locations. The primary caveat is severe operational constraints: the lobby is currently closed, there are zero ATMs, only one branch, and the institution's small size ($2.2M assets) may limit loan amounts, product sophistication, and service availability compared to regional or national credit unions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
